problem: Next work should be chosen by expected impact on the whole-game conformance score, not only by whichever local metric was most recently inspected.
strategy: Rank candidates by weighted overall-score lift, current gap, confidence, reuse, risk, and measured compute history.
successMeasure: Before long compute cycles, the selected work should have an explicit investment score and a measurable target/guardrail profile.

topCandidate.label: Advance audio cue contracts, reference segmentation, and cue matching
topCandidate.rationale: Audio is still the weakest runtime quality category, and the latest Challenge Perfect trial is now preserved as a rejected runtime decision: focused candidate perfect-onset-body-separated-soft-tail was useful generator evidence, but live/full-theme gates did not make it release-safe. Cue-contract readiness is 9.24/10; the next audio value is a safer candidate family, not direct promotion.
topCandidate.nextAction: Challenge Perfect runtime trial rejected perfect-onset-body-separated-soft-tail; do not directly promote the focused keeper. Do not promote Challenge Perfect runtime audio yet. Keep the new harness evidence, stabilize repeated full-theme capture, then compare bossHit, challengeTransition, and gameOver for the next best audio return.

candidates.label: Advance audio cue contracts, reference segmentation, and cue matching
candidates.rationale: Audio is still the weakest runtime quality category, and the latest Challenge Perfect trial is now preserved as a rejected runtime decision: focused candidate perfect-onset-body-separated-soft-tail was useful generator evidence, but live/full-theme gates did not make it release-safe. Cue-contract readiness is 9.24/10; the next audio value is a safer candidate family, not direct promotion.

candidates.label: Tighten level-arc opportunity coverage and late reward reads
candidates.rationale: The Stage 12 and Stage 14 learned reward routes are now credited; the Stage 14 sweep found 7 natural special-bonus routes, opportunity readiness is 7.8/10, and the next gap is closest-regular-stage-signature-gap.
candidates.nextAction: Use the top-ranked opportunity window to add or widen deterministic evidence before changing gameplay tuning.

candidates.label: Improve Stage 4 pressure exact replay
candidates.rationale: Level-arc pressure replay coverage remains a weak submetric, but prior cycles were more compute-heavy than the Stage 12 loop.
candidates.nextAction: Run focused source-window replay matching after the Stage 12 loop validates candidate mechanics.

candidates.label: Stage 1 opening timing polish
candidates.rationale: Stage 1 timing is a visible gap, but its current score is already reasonably high and the likely overall-score lift is smaller.
candidates.nextAction: Defer until higher-gap audio and level-arc candidates have been exercised.

candidates.label: Add frame-labeled Galaga boss and formation path references
candidates.rationale: Heuristic path-family comparison is now available and capped at 8.2/10 with confidence 0.8. The remaining path-shape gap is labeled Galaga reference path data, not runtime extraction.
candidates.nextAction: Label boss, escort, rack-settle, and challenge path families from Galaga reference contact sheets or video traces, then replace heuristic coverage with direct shape-distance scoring.

candidates.label: UI/graphics shell polish
candidates.rationale: Graphics integrity is important, but the current shell score is high; visual reference work should be planned after higher-gap axes.
candidates.nextAction: Defer unless new ingestion evidence reveals a larger graphics-conformance gap.

interpretation: Audio remains the largest raw gap and top overall investment. Semantic audio measurement debt is now 0 attention rows, segment-role comparisons are 31, the latest Challenge Perfect candidate decision is candidate-recommended, and the runtime-trial status is runtime-trial-rejected. If the next cycle stays in level-arc instead, the immediate level-arc task is closest-regular-stage-signature-gap.
